wrapUp: Harry Kane's late double rescued England from a shock Round of 32 exit against DR Congo in Atlanta, setting up a last-16 clash with co-hosts Mexico at the Estadio Azteca after Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez sent the hosts past Ecuador. Kylian Mbappe scored twice as France brushed Sweden aside 3-0 to join Norway, who claimed their first ever World Cup knockout win through Erling Haaland's late strike against Ivory Coast, in the last 16. Spain remain without Nico Williams and Yeremy Pino for Thursday's Austria tie, while Belgium face Senegal and the United States host Bosnia and Herzegovina later on 1 July. Each story below links to the specific report it is drawn from; squad and fitness details reflect the latest available reporting as of 1 July 2026.

sections.heading: Injuries and fitness doubts
sections.description: Players ruled out, late replacements and day-to-day concerns that could change starting line-ups and squad depth.

stories.headline: England miss James and Quansah at right-back for DR Congo tie
stories.summary: Reece James remained sidelined with a hamstring injury and Jarrell Quansah twisted an ankle in the Panama win as England entered their Round of 32 fixture without either of their preferred right-backs. Thomas Tuchel said both were getting closer to fitness but needed England to advance before they could return, leaving Djed Spence to cover the position against DR Congo. James was spotted speaking with Jude Bellingham during a hydration break in Atlanta, underlining how thin England's defensive options remain before the Mexico tie.

stories.headline: Williams and Pino ruled out as Spain prepare for Austria
stories.summary: Nico Williams and Yeremy Pino will both miss Spain's Round of 32 meeting with Austria on 2 July at SoFi Stadium after moderate injuries sustained against Uruguay. Williams suffered an adductor problem following Nicolas de la Cruz's challenge, while scans confirmed Pino's shoulder sprain is not a fracture but still rules him out of the Austria fixture. Luis de la Fuente may lean on Alex Baena and an increasingly fit Lamine Yamal out wide, with both wide men still doubts for a potential last-16 tie if Spain advance.

sections.heading: Squad selection and tactical reshuffles
sections.description: Omissions, late call-ups and manager decisions that reveal how teams plan to navigate the expanded format.

stories.headline: Kane double rescues England and sets up Mexico last-16 tie
stories.summary: Harry Kane headed Anthony Gordon's cross to equalise in the 75th minute and drilled a fierce strike into the roof of the net in the 86th to complete a 2-1 comeback against DR Congo in Atlanta on 1 July. Brian Cipenga had given the Leopards a shock seventh-minute lead, and Lionel Mpasi denied England repeatedly before Tuchel's side finally broke through. England will face co-hosts Mexico at the Estadio Azteca on 6 July, a fixture that will test whether the Three Lions can raise their level after a laboured knockout debut.

stories.headline: Mexico beat Ecuador to set up potential England quarter-final path
stories.summary: Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez scored either side of half-time as co-hosts Mexico beat Ecuador 2-0 at the Estadio Azteca on 1 July to reach the last 16 without conceding at the tournament. Kick-off was delayed by an hour because of storms in Mexico City, and Piero Hincapie was sent off in stoppage time after covering his mouth during an altercation with Santiago Gimenez. Mexico's reward is a home last-16 tie with England on 6 July, with altitude and crowd support giving Javier Aguirre's side a genuine edge against a team that struggled to break down DR Congo.

sections.heading: Disruption and preparation issues
sections.description: Travel, visa and logistical problems that could affect match readiness rather than just headlines.

stories.headline: USA target first knockout win since 2002 against Bosnia
stories.summary: The United States prepare for their Round of 32 tie with Bosnia and Herzegovina at Levi's Stadium on 1 July, seeking a first World Cup knockout victory since beating Mexico in 2002. Christian Pulisic said he felt good and ready to go after a calf concern limited him against Turkey, while Mauricio Pochettino apologised for his curt post-match tone following that defeat. The co-hosts have won two of three meetings with Bosnia at senior level but have only one victory in 21 games against European opposition since returning to the World Cup in 1990.

sections.heading: Analysis and tournament outlook
sections.description: Deeper previews on favourites, group dynamics and how the 48-team format changes the path to the final.

stories.headline: Belgium and Senegal meet in Seattle with knockout pedigree on show
stories.summary: Belgium topped Group G ahead of Egypt and face Senegal in Seattle on 1 July in one of the most evenly matched Round of 32 ties. Rudi Garcia said his side had achieved their minimum aim by escaping the group but wanted more, while Senegal arrive buoyed by a 5-0 win over Iraq that sent them through as a best third-placed finisher. Goalkeeper Edouard Mendy remains a doubt for Aliou Cisse's side after a knee issue against Norway, with Mory Diaw potentially continuing between the posts against Kevin De Bruyne and company.

stories.headline: Mbappe brace sends France past Sweden into Paraguay tie
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappe scored in each half as France beat Sweden 3-0 on 30 June at MetLife Stadium, with Bradley Barcola adding the second after Michael Olise's creative prompting. Mbappe moved level with Lionel Messi on six goals at this tournament and became the all-time leading scorer in World Cup knockout matches with 10, while France registered 25 shots in their most dominant display of the competition so far. Didier Deschamps's side face Paraguay in Philadelphia on 4 July, with Les Bleus now widely regarded as the team to beat after a third straight World Cup in which they have scored three or more in consecutive matches.

stories.headline: Haaland winner gives Norway first World Cup knockout victory
stories.summary: Erling Haaland prodded home from close range in the 86th minute as Norway beat Ivory Coast 2-1 on 30 June in Dallas to reach the last 16 for the first time in their history. The Manchester City striker has five goals at the tournament, and Stale Solbakken's side will face Brazil in New Jersey on 5 July after surviving a second half in which the Elephants pushed hard for an equaliser. Norway's path now runs through the form team of the knockout stage so far, with Carlo Ancelotti's Brazil having needed stoppage-time heroics to see off Japan.

stories.headline: Martinelli stoppage-time winner sends Brazil past Japan
stories.summary: Substitute Gabriel Martinelli scored in the fifth minute of added time as Brazil came from behind to beat Japan 2-1 on 29 June in Houston, denying Hajime Moriyasu's side a first-ever World Cup knockout victory. Kaishu Sano had put Japan ahead before Casemiro equalised with a header from Gabriel Magalhaes's cross, and Bruno Guimaraes slipped in Martinelli for the decisive finish. Brazil will face Norway in the Round of 16 on 5 July in New Jersey.

stories.headline: Paraguay eliminate Germany on penalties in Boston shock
stories.summary: Paraguay knocked four-time champions Germany out of the World Cup on 29 June, winning 4-3 on penalties after a 1-1 draw at Boston Stadium in Germany's first knockout defeat via a shootout. Jose Canale converted the decisive spot-kick after Jonathan Tah blazed over, capping a defiant display in which Paraguay completed just 161 passes to Germany's 719. Julian Nagelsmann's side failed to keep a clean sheet in any of their four matches, and Paraguay now face France in Philadelphia on 4 July.

stories.headline: Saibari sends Morocco past Netherlands in Monterrey shootout
stories.summary: Morocco beat the Netherlands 3-2 on penalties on 29 June after Issa Diop's stoppage-time header forced extra time in a 1-1 draw at Estadio Monterrey. Yassine Bounou saved Crysencio Summerville's fourth kick before Ismael Saibari sealed the win, sending Walid Regragui's side into a last-16 meeting with Canada in Houston on 4 July. The result marked the earliest World Cup exit for the Dutch since 1990 despite Cody Gakpo giving Ronald Koeman's team the lead in the second half.
